description: Shared foundation for the domain rulebooks (financial.json, land_title.json, legal_contract.json). The deterministic Layer-4/5/6 engine loads this once and merges it with each domain file. Value types define validation + extraction/match semantics; check_kinds is the FINITE, auditable catalog of deterministic primitives each rule dispatches to (no arbitrary expression evaluation). Governed by CLAUDE.md and ADR-004 (the model reads, deterministic rules decide).

status_vocabulary.PASS: invariant holds over the present claims
status_vocabulary.FAIL: invariant violated; localizes the offending field(s) + bbox as tamper evidence
status_vocabulary.NOT_APPLICABLE: rule does not apply to this document type
status_vocabulary.NOT_EVALUATED: required claims absent / not cross-read / below confidence gate — honest pending, never a pass

extraction_classes.cross_read_critical: a numeric whose VLM read MUST be confirmed by the deterministic OCR cross-read (ADR-004 section 5); disagreement -> the claim is NOT_EVALUATED. These are the numbers a forger edits.
extraction_classes.validated: a deterministic validator decides correctness (regex / checksum / calendar)
extraction_classes.fuzzy: compared with normalization + Levenshtein/rapidfuzz, never bit-exact

ParcelId.note: ULPIN / Khata / Khasra token; exact format is state-scoped (see coverage gates).

AreaUnit.calibration: bigha and ankanam vary by state/region; left null and treated as NOT_EVALUATED until a state-specific value is configured (honest gate, CLAUDE.md 3.4).

severity_bands._calibration: DEFAULT — needs calibration on a labeled corpus (CLAUDE.md 5). Values are suspicion in [0,1] for the risk engine; review_only never reaches APPROVE/REJECT on its own.

field_ref_grammar.description: How a rule binds to claim-graph values. A ref is one of the forms below; the engine resolves it against the claim graph for the document/bundle under evaluation.

series.comment: resolves to the ordered list of that field across all instances (e.g. every Transaction.credit)

linear_balance.semantics: running = anchor; for each row: expected = running + credit - debit; if printed present, compare expected to printed (FAIL+localize on mismatch) then re-anchor running on printed (so one edit breaks locally, not cascading) else running = expected

sum_equals.semantics: sum over the series == value(equals) within tolerance

sequence_monotonic.semantics: the series is non-decreasing (strict=false) or strictly increasing (strict=true)

words_equal_figures.semantics: parse Indian/English number-words in words_field to a Decimal and compare to figures_field within tolerance

fuzzy_match.semantics: normalized similarity(left, right) >= min_ratio; anchor='surname' anchors on the last token

all_equal_fuzzy.semantics: every value in field_set pairwise matches >= min_ratio (e.g. a party name across recital/body/signature/schedule)

set_subset.semantics: every item in subset (by key) exists in superset

references_resolve.semantics: every reference token in ref_field resolves to an existing target_entity by target_key

presence_count.semantics: count of present instances of target meets min (and exact if given)

sequence_complete.semantics: the integer field forms a complete run start..N with no gaps and no duplicates

quantity_equal.semantics: value(left) == value(right) after normalizing both to the canonical unit of dimension

consistency_across.semantics: the same logical field agrees across the named document types present in the bundle (within-bundle corroboration)

table_lookup.semantics: resolve a rate/value from a named external table keyed by fields; used as an input to another check. If the table is not configured -> the dependent rule is NOT_EVALUATED (honest gate).

functions.amortize_emi: EMI = P*r*(1+r)^n / ((1+r)^n - 1), r = annual_rate/12/100, n = term_months

formula.semantics: compute function(inputs) and compare to observed within tolerance; NOT_EVALUATED if any input missing

result_contract.description: Each evaluated rule yields one record the orchestrator wraps into a LayerSignal (ADR-004). Fields:
result_contract.evidence: list of {entity, field, bbox, expected, printed} for FAIL localization
result_contract.reason: human-readable, templated from the rule's reason string
